page: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processors running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include bundled local NVMe storage and relies on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 19 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the m6a.12xlarge demonstrates strong performance in CPU-intensive tasks, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is highly efficient for small-block cached read operations, though uncached large-block reads and writes fall into the average tier. Cryptographic execution and extended instruction processing also register strong benchmark results.

Given its balanced resource allocation and dedicated CPU execution, the m6a.12xlarge offers a cost-effective profile for workloads requiring steady-state compute and memory density without the premium of specialized hardware. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, web serving, and database operations. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration makes it less optimal for high-speed local caching or heavy parallel graphics processing, but highly efficient for general enterprise applications, mid-sized redis deployments, and text-processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processors running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth for cached reads, and robust cryptographic processing. It is optimized for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including web serving, software compilation, database operations, and multi-threaded application hosting. This configuration offers a highly stable environment for applications requiring consistent compute density without specialized hardware overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processors, delivering balanced compute and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and dedicated AMD EPYC processors.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-capacity DDR4 memory for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores of dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processors.
Equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz.
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps with no local storage.
Delivers strong multi-core CPU and cached memory read performance.
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
